Table 6: VBench-Long evaluation across all dimensions for Rolling Forcing. Comparison of Rolling Forcing with TTOM and CVG.
Table 1: VBench summary for short- and long-video generation. Comparison of base models with TTOM and CVG for short videos, and Rolling Forcing with TTOM and CVG for long videos.

Figure 5: User study results. Human preference comparison between CVG and TTOM. Participants compare generated videos along text alignment, motion, quality, and compositionality, and select whether CVG, TTOM, or both are better.

Figure 2: Overview of CVG. At inference time, CVG extracts subject-token cross-attention maps from a frozen text-to-video diffusion model and feeds them to a lightweight composition classifier. The classifier predicts the current compositional relation, and its loss with respect to the target compositional relation is backpropagated to update the latent. This provides composition-aware guidance without fine-tuning the generator or requiring external layout controls.
Table 2: Composition-aware evaluation across all dimensions. Comparison of base models with TTOM and CVG on T2V-CompBench (55).

Figure 3: Training compositional classifier. Given a real video and its prompt, we invert the video into the latent space of the frozen text-to-video model and extract subject-token cross-attention maps. These maps are processed by an aggregation module, a frozen VLM, and a trainable classification head to predict the target compositional relation label using cross-entropy supervision.
Table 4: VBench evaluation across all dimensions for Wan2.2-5B. Comparison of the base model with TTOM and CVG.
Figure 6: Additional Qualitative Results comparison between CVG and Wan2.2-14B (right), and between CVG and CogVideoX-5B (left).
Figure 4: Qualitative results. Text-to-video results before and after applying CVG on Wan2.2-14B (58) and CogVideoX5B (22). CVG strictly adheres to compositional instructions.
Table 5: VBench evaluation across all dimensions for Wan2.2-14B. Comparison of the base model with TTOM and CVG.
